review_meta.reviewer: sonnet (reviewer role, distinct from fixer a04893f and its plan-reviewer)
review_meta.review_worktree: .claude/worktrees/agent-a04893fbbc034ca4f (fixer's own worktree, updated to f1f6908 by the fixer; .env temporarily copied in + node_modules symlinked in for this review's test runs only, removed again after each run, never committed)
review_meta.method: git diff 1c5e909^ 1c5e909 read in full for the original review; git diff 1c5e909 f1f6908 read in full for the re-verify; fresh pytest + vitest + tsc + next build runs against the exact commits; grep-verified advisory-lock registry across apps/api/app; fail-before/pass-after proof for the new insights test by temporarily reverting email_agent.py to the pre-fix content, re-running, then restoring (file diff confirmed clean afterward).

verdict_history: FAIL at 1c5e909 (2 blocking findings below), PASS at f1f6908 (both findings corrected and independently re-verified with fresh evidence)
no_fabricated_scores_detail: TRUE for BOTH score surfaces as of f1f6908. (1) Triage->inbox list: EmailAgent._coerce_score (apps/api/app/agents/email_agent.py:152-166) rejects bool/non-numeric/missing and returns None, never 0; _triage only writes aiScore when non-None; GET /workspaces/emails/inbox surfaces `t.get("aiScore")`, genuinely NULL pre-triage -- unchanged from the original PASS-worthy finding, re-confirmed green. (2) On-demand insights ('Analyze this thread'/'Re-analyze'): _insights (apps/api/app/agents/email_agent.py, corrected line, was :361) now reads `"score": self._coerce_score(raw.get("score"))` instead of `int(raw.get("score", 0) or 0)` -- confirmed via git diff 1c5e909 f1f6908. A missing/non-numeric LLM score now yields insights["score"] = None, matching the triage path's discipline exactly. Verified genuine (not tautological) with a fail-before/pass-after proof: temporarily reverted email_agent.py to the 1c5e909 (pre-fix) content in the fixer's own worktree, re-ran tests/test_mv_email_center.py::test_insights_never_fabricates_score, and it FAILED with `assert 0 is None` (AssertionError) -- proving the test genuinely exercises the missing-score branch and that the old code really did fabricate a 0. Restored the corrected file (git diff against f1f6908 clean afterward) and re-ran the full suite -- 45/45 pass, all green.
cross_tenant_isolated_detail: Unchanged by the correction commit (git diff 1c5e909 f1f6908 touches only email_agent.py::_insights, background_jobs.py comment/ledger, gmail_service.py lock constant, and the new test -- no changes to _email_activity_stats or the inbox query). Re-confirmed: every subquery in _email_activity_stats (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py) is scoped `WHERE "userId" = %(uid)s`; recruiterEmails derives from an already-user-scoped thread list. test_inbox_stats_are_user_scoped (REV-B2) re-ran and PASSED in the 45-test run against f1f6908.
draft_reachable_detail: Unchanged by the correction commit. generateDraft() -> POST /agents/email/run mode=draft_reply -> EmailAgent._compose_draft (untouched), grounded + FabricationGuard-checked; POST /emails/send untouched, still honestly 409s with no fake 'sent' status.
migration_additive_only_detail: 0024_email_thread_ai_score.sql unchanged (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS, nullable) -- still additive-only. The correction commit fixes the ONE real defect found in the migration's supporting DDL-guard: the advisory-lock id.

lock_collision_fix_verified.change: apps/api/app/services/gmail_service.py:31 -- `_EMAIL_AI_COLS_LOCK` renamed from `7420240717` to `7420240723`; comment (lines 26-29) rewritten to point at the background_jobs.py ledger instead of re-asserting a standalone (and wrong) distinctness claim.
lock_collision_fix_verified.uniqueness_check: grep -rn "7420240723" apps/api/app --include="*.py" in the fixer's worktree (at f1f6908) returns exactly ONE hit: gmail_service.py:31. grep -rn "742024[0-9]*" across apps/api/app on current main (pre-branch baseline) tops out at 7420240722 -- 723 does not exist anywhere in main, confirming it is a genuinely fresh id, not a re-collision.
lock_collision_fix_verified.ledger_check: apps/api/app/repositories/background_jobs.py:22-27 now reads '...717; gmail_account 718; billing 719; admin 721; background_jobs 722; EmailThread aiScore (gmail_service) 723. Next genuinely-free id: 724.' -- accurately reflects both the historical entries and the new one, and the 'next free' pointer is correctly bumped from 722 to 724 (since 722 and 723 are now both taken). gmail_service.py's own comment (lines 26-29) now says 'See the lock-id ledger in app/repositories/background_jobs.py -- 717 is taken (user_provider_credential) and 722 (background_jobs), so the aiScore lock uses the next free id, 723' -- this is accurate and, unlike the original, does not assert distinctness in isolation without pointing at the authoritative source.
lock_collision_fix_verified.result: VERIFIED — 723 is unique repo-wide and the ledger + comment are now accurate.

backend_tests_result.reverify_command: cd apps/api && DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL_TEST AETHER_CREDENTIAL_KEY=<redacted> AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=false flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock python3 -m pytest tests/test_mv_email_center.py tests/test_email_agent.py tests/test_email_send_gate.py tests/test_emails.py tests/test_gap_p7_def_b_email_validation.py tests/test_workspaces.py -q
backend_tests_result.result: 45 passed, 0 failed, 7 warnings (deprecation only) in ~80s (against f1f6908)
backend_tests_result.new_test_only: tests/test_mv_email_center.py::test_insights_never_fabricates_score -- PASSED against f1f6908; FAILED (assert 0 is None) when email_agent.py was temporarily reverted to the pre-fix 1c5e909 content, proving the test is genuine (not a tautology) and exercises a missing-score (`{"breakdown": [...], "summary": "..."}` with no `score` key) LLM response via a _FakeLLM stub.
backend_tests_result.full_new_suite: tests/test_mv_email_center.py alone: 7 passed (the original 6 + the new insights test)

vitest_result: Not re-run for this correction (correction commit touches only apps/api/*.py + 1 backend test file per git diff 1c5e909 f1f6908 --stat; no frontend files changed). Original review's full-suite vitest/tsc/next-build results (385/385, tsc clean, build clean) stand unaffected.
reasons: Both blocking findings from the prior review are corrected and independently re-verified with fresh evidence in this run: (1) apps/api/app/agents/email_agent.py _insights now uses self._coerce_score(raw.get("score")) — genuine null-safety proven via fail-before/pass-after test run, not just code inspection. (2) apps/api/app/services/gmail_service.py:31 _EMAIL_AI_COLS_LOCK is now 7420240723, confirmed unique repo-wide by grep, and the background_jobs.py ledger + gmail_service.py comment are both updated accurately.
git diff 1c5e909 f1f6908 --stat confirms the correction is minimal and scoped exactly to the 2 required fixes plus 1 new test and 1 comment/ledger update -- no scope creep, no unrelated changes.
No prohibited patterns introduced by the correction (no eslint-disable/@ts-ignore/broad any/Math.random/suppressed errors/--no-verify).

All previously-PASSED findings (cross-tenant isolation CRITICAL, inbox no-fabricated-0, draft reachability, migration additive-only, MV-003/004/007 UI fixes) are untouched by this correction commit and re-confirmed green in the 45-test re-run.

The new test (test_insights_never_fabricates_score) is genuine: it constructs a _FakeLLM that returns breakdown/summary but omits "score" entirely, and asserts insights["score"] is None -- verified to fail against the pre-fix code (assert 0 is None) and pass against the fix.
